2026-04-20T06:48:46.9768770Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9769325Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9769773Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9770142Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9770458Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9770695Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9770852Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9771450Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9771643Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9771888Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-20T06:48:46.9772387Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T06:48:46.9772649Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. 2026-04-20T06:51:51.2954586Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T06:49:56.576147631Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58355","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-20T06:51:51.2992534Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T06:49:56.908035123Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58355,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Update error handling docs for eager validation and structured error fields\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58341) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58341 introduced two major behavioral changes to the Responses server:\\n\\n1. **Eager pre-handler validation**: `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are now validated against the provider *before* `CreateAsync` is invoked. Invalid references now return HTTP 404/400 immediately instead of being silently ignored. The resolved history items are cached so handlers that later call `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse the prefetched result.\\n\\n2. **Structured error field propagation**: `ResourceNotFoundException` gained `Code` and `Param` properties (mirroring `BadRequestException`). `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts all four fields (`message`, `code`, `param`, `type`) from Foundry error JSON and propagates them through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404 upstream error codes are normalized to HTTP 500 (never proxied).\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **README.md — Troubleshooting section** does not mention that invalid `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` values are rejected with 400/404 before the handler runs (users expect this to be silent or handler-handled)\\n- **README.md — Troubleshooting section** does not mention that non-400/404 errors from upstream Foundry storage are normalized to HTTP 500\\n- **docs/handler-implementation-guide.md — Handler Exceptions table** still shows `ResourceNotFoundException` → `error.code: null`, but it now carries `Code` and `Param` from the exception (matching `BadRequestException` behavior)\\n- **docs/handler-implementation-guide.md — Validation Pipeline section** does not document the new pre-handler validation step for `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit\\n\\nLocate the **Troubleshooting** section (~line 183) and extend the common-errors list to include the new pre-handler validation errors and the 500-normalization behavior:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Troubleshooting\\n\\n### Common errors\\n\\n- **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as `model` (when provided) are valid and that `input` items are well-formed.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (`conversation.id`): The `conversation.id` field referenced a conversation that the provider rejected as invalid. This validation runs before your handler is invoked.\\n- **404 Not Found** (`previous_response_id`): The `previous_response_id` field referenced a response that does not exist or has expired. This validation runs before your handler is invoked; the handler is never called when the reference is invalid.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response was not created with `background=true`, or it has already reached a terminal state.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel): The response was created with `store=false`, or a non-background response is still in-flight and not findable.\\n- **500 Internal Server Error**: An unexpected error occurred server-side. Errors from the upstream Foundry storage layer that are neither 400 nor 404 are always returned as HTTP 500 — the original upstream status code is never proxied to clients.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit\\n\\n**Change 1 — Handler Exceptions table**: Update the `ResourceNotFoundException` row to reflect that `Code` and `Param` are now propagated (lines around 1134):\\n\\nReplace the existing table:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.message` |\\n|---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|----------------|\\n| `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from exception (e.g., `\\u0026#34;invalid_value\\u0026#34;`) | from exception |\\n| `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | `null` | from exception |\\n| `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception |\\n| Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `\\u0026#34;server_error\\u0026#34;` | `\\u0026#34;An internal error occurred.\\u0026#34;` |\\n| `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* |\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.param` | `error.message` |\\n|---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|----------------|\\n| `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from `Code` (e.g., `\\u0026#34;invalid_value\\u0026#34;`) | from `ParamName` | from exception |\\n| `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | from `Code` | from `Param` | from exception |\\n| `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception | from exception |\\n| Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `\\u0026#34;server_error\\u0026#34;` | `null` | `\\u0026#34;An internal error occurred.\\u0026#34;` |\\n| `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* |\\n```\\n\\n**Change 2 — Validation Pipeline section**: Extend the section (~line ~1172) to document the new pre-handler validation:\\n\\nAfter the existing paragraph that begins _\\u0026#34;Bad client input returns HTTP 400 before your handler runs\\u0026#34;_, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n**Pre-handler reference validation**: In addition to payload schema validation, the library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the storage provider *before* `CreateAsync` is called:\\n\\n- An unrecognised `previous_response_id` causes the library to return HTTP 404 immediately; your handler is never invoked.\\n- An invalid `conversation.id` causes the library to return HTTP 400 immediately; your handler is never invoked.\\n\\nWhen the references are valid the resolved history items are cached, so `GetHistoryAsync()` inside your handler reuses the prefetched result without issuing a second storage call.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are needed for this change — the additions are prose and a table update.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Troubleshooting** section content needs updating.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README diffs from snippet injection\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24652253628/agentic_workflow) · ● 695.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58355\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T06:47:10Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T06:47:10Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

This validation runs before your handler is invoked.\\n- **404 Not Found** (`previous_response_id`): The `previous_response_id` field referenced a response that does not exist or has expired. This validation runs before your handler is invoked; the handler is never called when the reference is invalid.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response was not created with `background=true`, or it has already reached a terminal state.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel): The response was created with `store=false`, or a non-background response is still in-flight and not findable.\\n- **500 Internal Server Error**: An unexpected error occurred server-side. `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit\\n\\n**Change 1 — Handler Exceptions table**: Update the `ResourceNotFoundException` row to reflect that `Code` and `Param` are now propagated (lines around 1134):\\n\\nReplace the existing table:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.message` |\\n|---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|----------------|\\n| `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from exception (e.g., `\\u0026#34;invalid_value\\u0026#34;`) | from exception |\\n| `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | `null` | from exception |\\n| `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception |\\n| Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `\\u0026#34;server_error\\u0026#34;` | `\\u0026#34;An internal error occurred.\\u0026#34;` |\\n| `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* |\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.param` | `error.message` |\\n|---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|----------------|\\n| `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from `Code` (e.g., `\\u0026#34;invalid_value\\u0026#34;`) | from `ParamName` | from exception |\\n| `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | from `Code` | from `Param` | from exception |\\n| `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception | from exception |\\n| Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `\\u0026#34;server_error\\u0026#34;` | `null` | `\\u0026#34;An internal error occurred.\\u0026#34;` |\\n| `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* |\\n```\\n\\n**Change 2 — Validation Pipeline section**: Extend the section (~line ~1172) to document the new pre-handler validation:\\n\\nAfter the existing paragraph that begins _\\u0026#34;Bad client input returns HTTP 400 before your handler runs\\u0026#34;_, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n**Pre-handler reference validation**: In addition to payload schema validation, the library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the storage provider *before* `CreateAsync` is called:\\n\\n- An unrecognised `previous_response_id` causes the library to return HTTP 404 immediately; your handler is never invoked.\\n- An invalid `conversation.id` causes the library to return HTTP 400 immediately; your handler is never invoked.\\n\\nWhen the references are valid the resolved history items are cached, so `GetHistoryAsync()` inside your handler reuses the prefetched result without issuing a second storage call.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are needed for this change — the additions are prose and a table update.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Troubleshooting** section content needs updating.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README diffs from snippet injection\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-20T06:51:51.3185135Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T06:50:18.469393785Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":2,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4280857957,\"number\":58268,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document beta.2 features in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58252 and release commit cc01a6c by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58252 added the following significant features to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`, all released in 1.0.0-beta.2:\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key enforcement**: new `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header that, when supplied on `CreateResponse`, must be repeated on all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls. Mismatches silently return 404 for security isolation.\\n- **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: a new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n- **Metadata validation constraints**: metadata maps are now validated for ≤ 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 chars, value length ≤ 512 chars.\\n- **Malformed response ID validation**: both `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are validated for format; malformed values return 400.\\n- **Error response shape corrections**: cancel-after-delete now returns 404 (was 400); `invalid_request_error` type is now used consistently.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Key concepts** section of the README has no mention of `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`. Users who need multi-tenant isolation have no guidance on how to use it or what the header enforcement contract is.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Logging** section says only \\\"The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\\" It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or how to enable logging of outbound Foundry storage calls for diagnostics.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Common errors** section incorrectly says cancel returns `400 Bad Request` for terminal-state responses, and doesn't mention that cancel-after-delete returns **404**. It also doesn't mention the new **400** for malformed response IDs.\\n- No documentation exists for the metadata constraints (16-key, 64/512-char limits) that now produce validation errors.\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n#### 1a. Add a \\\"Chat isolation\\\" subsection under Key concepts\\n\\nAfter the existing `### Thread safety` subsection, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat isolation\\n\\nThe `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enables multi-tenant isolation. When a response is\\ncreated with this header, all subsequent operations for that response — GET, Cancel, DELETE, and\\nInputItems — must supply the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns `404 Not Found`\\n(indistinguishable from a non-existent response) so that cross-tenant enumeration is not possible.\\n\\nWhen no isolation key is set at creation time, no enforcement is applied to subsequent calls.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Common errors section\\n\\nReplace the existing bullet list with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Common errors\\n\\n- **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as\\n  `model` (when provided) are valid, that `input` items are well-formed, that `metadata` contains\\n  ≤ 16 keys with key length ≤ 64 characters and value length ≤ 512 characters, and that any\\n  supplied `previous_response_id` matches the expected ID format.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The `response_id` path parameter or the `previous_response_id`\\n  request body field does not match the expected format (prefix `caresp_` followed by 50 characters).\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state\\n  (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). The response body contains an\\n  `invalid_request_error` error type with the message `\\\"Cannot cancel a response in terminal state.\\\"`.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL, was\\n  created with `store=false`, or was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` that does not\\n  match the key provided in this request.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel or delete): Returned when the response does not exist *or* has\\n  already been deleted — cancel-after-delete returns 404 per spec.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section\\n\\nReplace the existing paragraph under `### Logging` with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\n\\n#### Foundry storage call logging\\n\\nWhen the Responses library is backed by Azure AI Foundry storage (`FoundryStorageProvider`),\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound HTTP call at `Information` level on success and\\n`Warning`/`Error` level on failures. Each log entry includes:\\n\\n- HTTP method and URI\\n- HTTP status code and latency\\n- Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id` (outbound), `x-ms-request-id`,\\n  `x-request-id`, and `apim-request-id` (inbound from Foundry)\\n\\nTo enable these logs, configure the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging `Information` level for\\n`Azure.AI.AgentServer` in your `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"Logging\\\": {\\n    \\\"LogLevel\\\": {\\n      \\\"Azure.AI.AgentServer\\\": \\\"Information\\\"\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for these changes — the documentation additions are\\nexplanatory prose, not code samples. However, if a sample demonstrating chat isolation is\\ndesired, add a new test file:\\n\\n**Path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample5_ChatIsolation.cs`\\n\\nWrap the illustrative code in `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion`\\nand place the corresponding snippet placeholder in the README's new \\\"Chat isolation\\\" subsection.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all\\nrequired sections. The changes above update existing sections and add one new subsection; no\\nsections need to be created from scratch.\\n\\nCurrent structure (all present):\\n1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — needs \\\"Chat isolation\\\" subsection added\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — needs Logging and Common errors updated\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if new snippet regions were added\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e [!TIP]\\n\\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u003e Generated by [Update
